steps.title: Require both places
steps.content: The claim is specific: the quotation must use figurative language to connect the narrator's urban environment of Washington, DC, with the rural South of the narrator's past. Evidence that names only one place, or that lacks figurative language, will not fully illustrate that claim.

steps.title: Reject partial evidence
steps.content: Choice A uses a simile for the Capitol dome but stays within Washington, DC. Choice B uses figurative language about train sounds but does not connect DC to the South. Choice D mentions V Street in Washington, DC, but gives no figurative link to the narrator's Southern homeland. The answer is C.
